sn14.14:1.1: At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, it is by way of elements that beings come together and unite. Those of an inferior disposition come together and unite with those of an inferior disposition; those of a good disposition come together and unite with those of a good disposition. [n.242] The contrast is between hinādhimuttikā and kalyāṇādhimuttikā. Spk glosses adhimuttikā with ajjhāsaya, "inclination."
sn14.14:2.1: In the past, by way of elements, beings came together and united … .
sn14.14:3.1: In the future, too, by way of elements, beings will come together and unite … . sn.ii.155
sn14.14:4.1: Now too, at present, by way of elements, beings come together and unite. Those of an inferior disposition come together and unite with those of an inferior disposition; those of a good disposition come together and unite with those of a good disposition."
